title: US, Iran agree to halt attacks as fragile ceasefire holds; Qatar to host Hormuz talks on Tuesday
content: Amidst escalating tensions, the United States and Iran have agreed to a ceasefire and will resume talks in Doha, Qatar, to de-escalate the dispute over the Strait of Hormuz.  Following days of military exchanges, both nations will halt counter-strikes, allowing vessels to navigate freely. The discussions aim to resolve differing interpretations of a recent memorandum of understanding concerning maritime passage and port access.

title: India crash out of Women's T20 WC despite Harmanpreet's heroics
content: Australia knocked India out of the Women's T20 World Cup with a comfortable six-wicket win, thanks to a match-winning 100-run partnership between Ellyse Perry (56) and Ashleigh Gardner (53*). Earlier, Harmanpreet Kaur's explosive 56 off 27 balls helped India post 170/4, but the total proved insufficient as Australia advanced to the semifinals unbeaten.

title: Jeff Bezos vs Zohran Mamdani debate: Kevin O’Leary says Amazon founder should run cities
content: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos sparked a debate on government efficiency and taxation, suggesting New York City's services would falter under his company's operational model. Billionaire Kevin O'Leary echoed these sentiments, calling the city a 'disaster' and warning against taxing the wealthy. Former Mayor Bill de Blasio criticized Bezos' remarks as out of touch. Meanwhile, Bezos also dismissed fears of AI causing job losses, predicting a labor shortage instead.

title: Michael Dell updates on Dell's Texas move days after Abbott's 'welcome home' message
content: Dell Technologies is officially moving its legal base from Delaware to Texas, with 97% of shareholders approving the change. CEO Michael Dell expressed pride in returning the company's legal home to the state where it was founded. This move aligns with Texas's growing appeal for major corporations, including Tesla, Coinbase, and Exxon Mobil, due to its favorable business climate and talent pool.

title: Land donated for $10 as a park sold for $10 million to build a data centre
content: A Texas city sold 87 acres donated in 1999 for a $10 public park to a data center developer for $10 million. Residents, led by Pamela Griffin, are suing to honour the original deed, while Taylor officials say existing zoning permits the data center without resident approval. The project promises $30 million in tax revenue but raises concerns over noise, water use and property values.

title: Erling Haaland and Isabel Johansen have built a massive 2026 fortune
content: Erling Haaland and Isabel Haugseng Johansen have built one of football's most talked-about partnerships. While Haaland's estimated $100 million net worth comes from his Manchester City contract, bonuses and endorsements, Johansen maintains a private lifestyle with an estimated net worth of $250,000-$300,000. The couple welcomed their first son in 2024 and continue to enjoy a family-focused life. Their combined wealth, luxury properties and growing global profile keep them among football's most prominent young couples.

title: Leanna Lenee's viral tattoo video sparks fresh backlash from NFL fans
content: Travis Hunter's wife, Leanna Lenee, is facing fresh online backlash after a viral video about the Jacksonville Jaguars star's chest tattoo reignited debate over their relationship. The clip spread quickly across social media, prompting fans to revisit past controversies and post harsh reactions, with some even predicting the couple's future. Hunter has defended Leanna publicly in the past, but the latest video has once again put the spotlight on the pair. Here's what happened and why the internet is talking about them again.

title: Insider claims Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce may have already exchanged vows
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have fueled fresh wedding speculation after an insider claimed the couple may have already exchanged vows ahead of their rumored July 3 ceremony at Madison Square Garden. While neither has confirmed the report, fans continue to debate the possibility online. The couple reportedly scheduled the celebration around Kelce's NFL offseason, with plans to postpone an extended honeymoon so he can prepare for the Kansas City Chiefs' upcoming season.

title: J&K: 31-year-old Pakistani national held after crossing LoC in Poonch
content: Jammu and Kashmir Police apprehended a 31-year-old Pakistani national, Rayees Khan, on Sunday after he allegedly crossed the Line of Control into Indian territory in the Balakote sector. No incriminating material was found. This marks the third such apprehension this month, following the capture of another Pakistani national, Mohd Sajad, on Friday near the LoC in Poonch. Both individuals are currently being questioned to determine their motives and backgrounds.

title: 'Babur Janata Party': Uddhav Thackeray attacks BJP over Ram Mandir donation row
content: Shiv Sena (UBT) chief Uddhav Thackeray slammed the BJP over alleged Ram Mandir donation irregularities, dubbing them the "Babur Janata Party" and accusing them of exploiting faith for political power. He also alleged the BJP is weakening Shiv Sena to undermine. Referring to BJP's long-standing slogan, he questioned the party's intentions behind the construction of the Ram temple.

title: Ireland shock India again, seal first-ever T20I series win over world champions
content: Ireland achieved a historic one-run victory over reigning T20 World Cup champions India, securing a stunning 2-0 series sweep. Debutant Jai Moondra's fiery opening spell and disciplined death bowling proved crucial as Ireland defended a modest 154. Despite Tilak Varma's valiant 55, India's chase faltered, ending their impressive 16-series winning streak and exposing familiar batting frailties.

title: Ketan murder case: Police seize Chetan's 2-wheeler used to 'reach' Lohagad Fort
content: Police have seized a motorcycle, hoodie, and headphones allegedly used by Chetan Chaudhary in connection with the murder of businessman Ketan Agarwal at Lohagad Fort.  Accused Siya Goyal and Chaudhary were taken to the fort to reconstruct the crime scene, where Agarwal allegedly fell to his death.  The investigation, initially an accidental fall, is now a murder case.

title: 'SUV, Rs 65L house': How Ram Temple donation theft accused 'upgraded' his lifestyle
content: Investigators are scrutinizing the financial dealings and assets of Anukalp Mishra, a key suspect in the alleged embezzlement of Ram Temple donations. Police are examining his reportedly improved lifestyle, recent property acquisitions, and a lavish religious event to establish a money trail. His brother-in-law, Lavkush Mishra, also arrested, is under similar financial scrutiny.

title: Ben Stokes announces retirement from Tests, shocks cricket world
content: The announcement came while Stokes was in the middle of a bowling spell. England Cricket released a statement confirming that the 35-year-old would step away from international cricket after the conclusion of the Test.

title: Why India's AMCA still needs a US engine, and so do many of the world's fighters
content: India's ambitious fifth-generation fighter jet program faces a significant hurdle as the cost of crucial US-made engines has reportedly tripled, jeopardizing timelines and budgets. This reliance on foreign propulsion systems, also impacting the Tejas Mk2, highlights a persistent challenge in developing world-class fighter jet engines domestically. While other nations also depend on foreign engines initially, India's pursuit of strategic autonomy hinges on mastering this complex technology.

title: Deepti scripts history despite India's T20 WC exit, becomes highest ever to take...
content: Deepti Sharma became the highest wicket-taker in women's international cricket, surpassing Jhulan Goswami's record with the dismissal of Australia's Beth Mooney during India's must-win Women's T20 World Cup clash. Earlier, captain Harmanpreet Kaur's explosive 56 off 27 balls, including three consecutive sixes in the final over, helped India post a competitive 170/4.

title: How Telegram’s NEET controversy reveals a bigger battle over coaching, piracy and access
content: Telegram's temporary ban, linked to NEET exam leaks, sparks debate on its role in India's shadow education economy. While students utilize its features for affordable learning, the platform also facilitates the free circulation of paid educational content, leading to copyright battles. Critics argue the ban punishes legitimate users and fails to address the root cause of leaks, highlighting a complex challenge for policymakers balancing intellectual property with educational access.

title: Khamenei says US-Israel ‘boasting’ over Iran strikes amounts to confession of crimes
content: Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei has demanded legal action against US and Israeli leaders for alleged war crimes, citing the deaths of schoolchildren in Minab. He views their public statements as admissions of guilt, urging domestic and international courts to pursue accountability. This comes as Iran's foreign ministry condemned recent US airstrikes on its southern coast, with both nations exchanging retaliatory actions and accusations.

title: 'Disqualify six rebel MPs': Uddhav links defections to 'Operation Devendra'
content: Shiv Sena (UBT) chief Uddhav Thackeray demanded the disqualification of six rebel MPs, calling their defection a "larger political conspiracy" and part of "Operation Devendra." He urged Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla to uphold the rule of law, alleging the BJP is undermining its own leaders. Thackeray, on a tour of rebel MPs' constituencies, asserted that people voted against the "Modi wave," not for the defectors.

title: Nasa launches high-stakes mission to save ageing telescope Swift from falling to Earth
content: The mission will be carried out by US startup Katalyst Space Technologies, which won the Nasa contract last September. Its three-armed robotic spacecraft, named Lift, will launch aboard an air-launched Pegasus rocket from the Marshall Islands before spending about a month chasing down Swift in orbit. Swift has been observing the universe since 2004.

title: 13-year-old 'raped' by over 12 men for 4 days in Rajasthan; 10 arrested
content: A 13-year-old girl was allegedly sexually exploited by nearly a dozen individuals over four days in Sriganganagar, sparking outrage. The victim was reportedly taken to multiple hotels after seeking a ride home. Police have arrested 10 people, including hotel staff and an e-rickshaw driver, and are investigating further. DVR footage from four hotels is being analyzed to identify more suspects.

title: Drama! Smriti, Jemimah involved in heated staredown - Watch
content: Tempers flared during India's Women's T20 World Cup clash against Australia after a mix-up between close friends Smriti Mandhana and Jemimah Rodrigues led to Mandhana's run-out. The pair exchanged angry looks before Mandhana later brushed off the incident. Meanwhile, captain Harmanpreet Kaur's explosive 56 off 27 balls powered India to a competitive total of 170/4.

title: Odisha: Four minors attack catering staff for not serving mutton curry at wedding feast
content: Four teenagers were arrested in Odisha's Balasore district for allegedly assaulting a catering staff member. The incident occurred at a wedding feast when the victim reportedly refused to serve mutton, offering chicken instead. The dispute escalated, leading to the minors allegedly attacking the caterer with a sharp weapon, causing severe injuries.

title: Skydiving tragedy in France: Five students among 11 killed in plane crash
content: A source close to the investigation said the victims were part of a group of nursing students. The aircraft, which was registered in Germany, crashed into a grassy area near the runway at Nancy-Essey Aerodrome in eastern France. The crash site was located close to a residential neighbourhood and two roads, according to an AFP journalist at the scene.

title: Kerala government orders probe after protocol lapse causes Vijayan to miss flight
content: Kerala's opposition leader, Pinarayi Vijayan, missed his flight from Delhi to Kerala due to a protocol lapse. Officials failed to confirm travel plans with the airline, despite Vijayan reaching the airport well in advance. An inquiry has been ordered, with disciplinary action promised against the responsible protocol staff. Vijayan eventually travelled to Kannur on a later flight.

title: NFL trade rumors: Buffalo Bills linked with $6 million Denver Broncos WR ahead of 2026 season
content: The Buffalo Bills have emerged as a potential landing spot for Denver Broncos wide receiver Marvin Mims Jr. , whose future is uncertain after Denver acquired Jaylen Waddle. With Josh Allen entering his ninth NFL season, Buffalo could look to add another explosive playmaker. Mims admitted frustration with his role but remains professional, while analysts believe his speed and upside make him an intriguing trade candidate ahead of the 2026 season.

title: 'It's about creating spaces for girls': NBA’s long game beyond superstars
content: From the WPL to the WNBA, women's sport has entered a new era of growth. Yet, for Lauren Jackson, the bigger challenge is ensuring girls stay in sport long enough to become players, coaches and leaders. Speaking on the NBA's Her Time To Play leadership panel in Singapore, the Australian great argued that sustaining women's sport begins long before the professional stage.

title: Father 'forgives' girlfriend who kicked his 18-month-old toddler to death, sparks outrage in China
content: A father in China has sparked national fury by forgiving his girlfriend accused of fatally kicking their 18-month-old son. The child's mother, the ex-wife, demanded a thorough investigation after discovering the toddler's severe abdominal injuries, which led to his death. Authorities revealed the girlfriend allegedly inflicted the fatal blows, while the father claims he believed she was caring for the child.

title: Female cadet pilot injured by running propeller at Kanpur’s flight training institute
content: A cadet pilot from Delhi sustained a lower back injury after being hit by a propeller while disembarking a training aircraft in Kanpur. The incident occurred when the 22-year-old reportedly exited the Tecnam P2006T before both engines were fully shut down. The DGCA has launched an inquiry, the instructor has been grounded, and the aircraft is out of service pending investigation.

title: Bihar on verge of bankruptcy, says Tejashwi
content: While addressing the press at the party office, the Rashtriya Janata Dal (RJD) leader alleged that the state is facing extreme financial loss due to scams, corruption, revenue loss, widening fiscal deficit and mounting debt

title: MoU signed for indigenous chips in CCTV cameras
content: According to Mindgrove Technologies this is one of the largest commercial integrations of Indian-designed silicon to date

title: Hospital dedicated to geriatric care inaugurated in city
content: Karthik Narayan, founder and managing director of Athulya Senior Care, pointed out that India has only around 20,000 dedicated senior care beds, compared with nearly eight million in China and close to three million in the U.S.

title: 1,747 out-of-school children in Yadgir to be brought back to classrooms through NIOS Mitra
content: NIOS Mitra is a technology-enabled community outreach initiative launched by the Ministry of Education to identify, counsel and enrol OoSCs into the formal education system, particularly those in the 14-18 age group

title: Minister for Cooperatives inspects renovated wholesale market on Bazaar Road in Arakkonam
content: Officials of the Arakkonam Municipality, which executed the ₹6-crore work, say this is the second time the market complex was renovated since 1984

title: Landslide dams river in rain-battered Arunachal, warning for Assam downstream
content: Lower Siang district officials say the possibility of a powerful flash flood if the dam breaks could endanger homes along the Siji river, which becomes the Gai river in Assam

title: MEA issues advisory for citizens undertaking Mansarovar Yatra through private tour operators
content: The Mansarovar Yatra resumed last year after a gap of nearly five years as part of efforts to normalise the relations between India and China

title: Stalin accuses TVK-led government of not addressing power cuts, farmers’ problems
content: According to him, industrial establishments are hesitant to invest in the State due to the deterioration of law and order, increasing sexual abuse cases, narcotic drug menace, and robberies

title: Villupuram MP reviews Central government schemes being implemented in the district at DISHA meeting
content: The allocation of funds under each scheme, the ongoing works, and the progress made, besides those that were completed, were also reviewed

title: Five-year-old girl, sleeping at home, found dead in a well in Ramanathapuram
content: Around 5.30 a.m. on Sunday, her mother woke up and found that her elder daughter, who had been sleeping beside her, was missing. On her complaint,  the police, along with Fire and Rescue Services, launched a search and found the body  the neighbour’s well. A case of suspicious death has been registered

title: CPI(M) protests inauguration of rafting event amid search for missing person
content: The party alleges that inaugurating the rafting event in a celebratory manner while an intensive search for the missing person was under way nearby was inhuman and shocking

title: UDF govt. set to privatise major sectors, says Pinarayi
content: Leader of the Opposition says the UDF government is reversing the State’s gains and that education, health, electricity and even Kerala’s ‘sea and sky’ are headed for privatisation

title: Sonam Wangchuk begins hunger strike at Jantar Mantar, six students join
content: Activist joins the CJP protest demanding the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan for multiple irregularities in the examination system

title: Uttar Pradesh police search residences of all accused in Ayodhya embezzlement case
content: Samajwadi Party president Akhilesh Yadav and Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath trade barbs as the row over alleged embezzlement of Ram Temple donations peaks

title: What does the Centre’s Vision Document on Drug Control 2026-2029 propose?
content: The three-year road map on drug control seeks to dismantle narcotics networks through intelligence-led enforcement, technology, preventive detention, rehabilitation, and coordinated action against inter-State and transnational drug cartels

title: Govt. rejects Pakistan’s allegations linking India to Karachi attack
content: Official spokesperson of the External Affairs Ministry Randhir Jaiswal said, “Pakistan would do better to look inwards, take credible actions (against) the terror infrastructure on its territory”.

title: Students stage protest against indiscriminate fee hike in KSLU
content: They highlight the need for corrective measures to resolving various issues affecting students in university in Hubballi

title: DGCA probes incident of trainee pilot getting injured by aircraft propeller at Kanpur airport
content: The incident occurred after the trainee pilot deboarded the twin-engine Tecnam P2006T aircraft VT-NBV, and the instructor concerned has been derostered from flying training duties pending investigation, DGCA said

title: Madras High Court denies anticipatory bail to suspended woman Inspector of Police in gold coin fraud case
content: Justice C. Kumarappan dismisses the advance bail petition filed by Sheela Mary, who had served at the Royapuram All-Women police station in Chennai in 2024

title: Over 2,500 visitors attend vegetable, seeds festival at VIT in Vellore
content: At the event, the Seed Conservator Awards-2026 were presented to nine farmers from six States in recognition of their efforts to conserve traditional seeds

title: IMD says wait for monsoon may be over in 5-6 days even as Delhi, Uttar Pradesh reel under heat
content: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) said in a release that conditions are favourable for further advance of the southwest monsoon into some more parts of the North Arabian Sea and some areas of Uttar Pradesh and Uttarakhand, among other States, during the next two to three days.

title: Uddhav Thackeray seeks disqualification of rebel MPs, says defections part of bigger conspiracy
content: MPs loyal to the Shiv Sena (UBT) had submitted a formal representation to Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla, seeking an opportunity to present their side, Uddhav Thackeray said, noting that party MP Arvind Sawant had cut short an official visit to Kargil after the Speaker had advanced the date of the hearing

title: Central Sanskrit University introduces B.Tech programme in AI, data science
content: Prime Minister Modi in his Mann Ki Baat address stated that this will facilitate development of new AI tools for Indian languages and accelerate digitisation of ancient texts

title: Talks fail to end nurses’ strike in two Thrissur hospitals
content: Hospitals say an immediate wage revision is not feasible, but they will abide by salary revision announced by State govt.; nurses have been on strike for three months in front of the two hospitals, demanding a minimum monthly salary of ₹40,000

title: "I Will Return To My Country This Year": Sheikh Hasina Exclusive
content: Any attack on the minorities is an attack on the independence of Bangladesh, stressed Sheikh Hasina in an interview with NDTV

title: Bill To Check Thugs In Bengal Assembly On Monday, Allows Preventive Detention
content: The Bill empowers the government to detain a person if it believes detention is necessary to stop future anti-social activity.

title: Qatari National Killed From Shrapnel Injuries Due To "Military Operation"
content: The ministry said the second individual was injured, adding that it located the missing vessel in the early hours of Sunday after search operations that started a day earlier.

title: US-Iran War Highlights: US-Iran Exchange Strikes, Deal Under Pressure
content: US-Iran War Highlights: The clashes brought new tension to the negotiating process meant to end a war launched by the United States and Israel at the end of February.

title: Man Hacked To Death On Bengaluru Street, Revenge Killing Suspected
content: The victim, who has a criminal background, was shifted to a private hospital in a critical condition, where he later succumbed to his injuries.

title: 2 Killed, 2 Injured In Lightning Strikes In Jharkhand
content: Two people, including a three-year-old girl, were killed and two others sustained burn injuries in incidents of lightning strikes in Jharkhand on Sunday, officials said.

title: How A Rats' Nest Led To Poison Pills At Mumbai's Muharram Procession
content: Police sources said Premji had conducted extensive research on Google and AI regarding the effects of various dosages of zinc phosphide on the human body and had easy access to Zinc Phosphide.

title: Second Cloud Burst Hits Arunachal Within A Week, Travel Advisories Issued
content: Torrential monsoon rains, followed by cloudbursts, have triggered severe flooding in the East Siang and Leparada districts of Arunachal Pradesh.

title: Tehran-Dubai Flight Services To Resume Tomorrow, Says Iranian Media
content: Dubai airport was repeatedly targeted by drones during the Middle East war, forcing it to suspend flights at times.

title: India's Big Step Towards Clean Fuel With Green Hydrogen From Nuclear Reactor
content: Hydrogen is widely seen as the fuel of the future, especially for sectors that are difficult to decarbonize. By producing hydrogen using nuclear heat, India can create large volumes of clean fuel without emitting carbon dioxide.

title: New York Judge's Query No Threat To Adani Group Case Dismissal: US Lawyer
content: A US federal judge's decision to seek a fuller explanation from prosecutors before approving the dismissal of criminal charges against Indian billionaire Gautam Adani is a procedural requirement, according to a senior US lawyer familiar with federal criminal practice.

title: Couple Killed, 4 Injured As Car Rams Stationary Truck In UP's Mirzapur: Cops
content: A married couple died, and four others were injured after their car rammed into a stationary truck on National Highway-135 here in the early hours of Sunday, police said.

title: "I'm Human": D Fadnavis After Uddhav Thackeray's "Clip Wings" Remark
content: "It is my habit that whenever I board a flight, I have something downloaded on my mobile. It could be a movie or a series on an OTT platform. I watch that. So, where exactly did he see my helplessness?" Devendra Fadnavis said

title: NASA's $30 Million Mission To Save Telescope From Falling Back To Earth
content: The $30 million salvage operation gets underway as soon as this week with the planned launch of a robotic lifesaver.

title: 19-Month-Old Taken To Madhya Pradesh Hospital With Cold. He Loses Eyesight
content: The family claims that during treatment, a medicine meant for clearing phlegm was allegedly put into the child's eyes.

title: Dance, Dive, Tragedy: Man Dies After Jumping Into Resort Pool In Maharashtra
content: A 25-year-old tourist died after diving headfirst into a shallow swimming pool at a resort in Maharashtra's Sindhudurg district, with a video of the fatal incident now circulating on social media.

title: PM Modi Highlights Climate Impact On Island Nations In Seychelles
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Sunday said the Global South, especially island nations, was the most impacted by climate change and called for climate action guided by fairness, responsibility and equity, in his address to the National Assembly of Seychelles.

title: '17 Years Of Silence': Baloch Activist's Open Letter Exposes Pak's Forced Disappearances
content: For years, Baloch nationalist groups, families of missing persons and international rights organisations have alleged that enforced disappearances have become a recurring feature of Pakistan's counter-insurgency operations

title: Kerala government replaces prosecutor in ED assault case amid bail controversy, appoints Special Public Prosecutor
content: Police allege that the prosecutor failed to effectively oppose the bail application, despite a detailed report recommending its rejection

title: Tiruppur woman dies after botched YouTube-assisted childbirth at home
content: K. Sasikala, of Punjaithalavaipalayam in Uthukuli taluk, was taken to a private hospital in Coimbatore, where she died on Sunday morning of post-partum haemorrhage and related complications

title: Off-roading is off limits: In a first, Ladakh fines tourists for driving into lake
content: four tourists fined a total of Rs 2 lakh for off-roading in ladakh's Pangong Lake . four vehicles used during the trips over the past fortnight were impounded . a viral video showed one of them purportedly chasing a Tibetan Gazelle through Changthang Cold Desert Wildlife Sanctuary .

title: The price tag on having fun: Why do hobbies feel more expensive than ever
content: some latest additions to this ever-diversifying social media dictionary include "maxxing", "locking in" and the most ubiquitous of them all - "grinding" these are not, on their face, alarming words . the culture of commodifying did not even spare the movies .

title: Who is the head of the family? A Census question that's making Indian families think twice
content: who is the head of your family? the question means different things to different people . for some families, the answer comes instinctively: the father or the grandfather . in many homes, everyone looks at each other before naming someone .

content: Anuja Jaiswal is a Senior Assistant Editor at The Times of India, with an impressive 18-year career in narrative journalism. She specializes in health and heritage reporting, expertly simplifying complex health information to make it engaging and understandable for readers. Her deep dives into heritage topics are well-researched, resulting in captivating narratives that resonate with her audience. Over the years, she has worked in Chandigarh, Chhattisgarh and West UP, gaining diverse on-ground experience that shapes her storytelling. Read More

title: India tops economic capacity, ranks 74th in workforce readiness
content: Manash Pratim Gohain is a seasoned journalist with over two decades at The Times of India, where he has built a rich body of work spanning education policy, politics, and governance. Renowned for his incisive coverage of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, accreditation reforms, and skilling initiatives, he has also reported on student politics, urban policy, and social movements. His political reportage—both reflective and news-driven—adds depth to his writing, bridging policy with public impact. Through his 2,500 articles and related outlets, he has emerged as a trusted voice in national discourse, particularly in linking education reform to broader societal change. Read More

title: PMML holds special event to mark birth anniversary of former PM Narasimha Rao
content: the exhibition at Pradhanmantri Sangrahalaya located in Teen Murti Bhavan will continue till June 30 . the exhibition showcases a collection of rare photographs, official documents, speeches, correspondence and archival materials .

title: Trainee pilot hurt by running propeller while leaving aircraft
content: trainee pilot from new Delhi was hit on the back by a still-running propeller . the 22-year-old suffered back injuries after deboarding a twin-engine trainer aircraft . DGCA has off-rostered the instructor from flying training duties pending the probe .

title: MEA rejects Pakistan claim of India’s hand in Karachi attack
content:
NEW DELHI: India has slammed Pakistan for alleging Indian involvement in an attack on a military base in Karachi Saturday, saying Pakistan needs to look inwards and dismantle terror infrastructure on its territory. Four paramilitary personnel were killed in a terrorist attack on a Sindh Rangers compound in Karachi Saturday night. It was the city’s first major terrorist strike since Oct 2024. Pakistani security forces killed six terrorists and captured one alive. In Karachi on Saturday, PM Shehbaz Sharif accused India of using “proxies” to undermine peace and stability in Pakistan. Ministry of external affairs spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal rejected the allegations as “baseless”. He said, “Instead of pointing fingers at others, Pakistan would do better to look inwards, take credible action against terror infrastructure...and rid itself of its proclivity to rely on terrorism as an instrument of state policy.

End of Article

title: Law must act as shield for innocent, not as a sword for the vindictive: SC
content: a bench of justices said trial proceedings cannot be allowed on the basis of vague statements and quashed a corruption case against a govt official in Odisha after noting there was no specific allegation against him . the law must act as shield for the innocent, not as a sword for the vindictive .

title: No pressure was put on Champat Rai to resign: VHP international president Alok Kumar
content: Manash Pratim Gohain is a seasoned journalist with over two decades at The Times of India, where he has built a rich body of work spanning education policy, politics, and governance. Renowned for his incisive coverage of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, accreditation reforms, and skilling initiatives, he has also reported on student politics, urban policy, and social movements. His political reportage—both reflective and news-driven—adds depth to his writing, bridging policy with public impact. Through his 2,500 articles and related outlets, he has emerged as a trusted voice in national discourse, particularly in linking education reform to broader societal change. Read More
